title: Optical fiber degradation monitoring by distributed temperature sensing
abstract: Aspects of the present disclosure describe monitoring of optical fiber by distributed temperature sensing (DTS) and determining optical fiber degradation and/or abnormal environmental events including landslides, fires, etc., from DTS data.
claims: Claims Claims: 1. A distributed temperature sensing (DTS) method for determining optical fiber degradation comprising: operating a DTS system to obtain temperature measurements at points along a fiber optic cable; determining daily temperature variability at each of the measurement points based on the temperature measurements; determining a fiber degradation indication from the daily temperature variability at the measurement points. 2. The method of claim 1 further comprising: determining an abnormal temperature indication from the daily temperature variability at the measurement points. 3. The method of claim 1 wherein the temperature measurements are obtained during a time period comprising a multiple of 24 hours. 4. The method claim 3 wherein the fiber degradation is caused by a bending of the fiber optic cable. 5. The method of claim 4 wherein the bending of the fiber optic cable is caused by shifting of a ground material in which the fiber optic cable is buried. 6. The method of claim 2 wherein the abnormal temperature indication is caused by a fire proximate to the fiber optic cable. 7. The method of claim 1 further comprising generating a report to a maintenance facility indicating the degraded condition and its location in the fiber optic cable. 8. The method of claim 6 further comprising generating a report to a maintenance facility indicating the abnormal temperature due to fire and location in the fiber optic cable. 9. The method of claim 1 further comprising: operating a Raman-based OTDR system; and determining any cable installation characteristics from Raman-based OTDR data. 10. The method of claim 9 further comprising: determining a loss indicative of a bad installation; and determining different types of optical fiber comprising the optical fiber cable.
